source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Disable IPv6 Networking if not in use within your organization.

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Disable IPv6 Networking if not in use within your organization.
input_row.description: Any protocols enable within Lightsail by default that aren't being used should be disabled.
input_row.rationale: Any ports enable within Lightsail by default are open and exposed to the world. This can result in outside traffic trying to access or even deny access to the Lightsail instances. Removing and disabling a protocol when not in use even if restricted by IP address is the safest solution especially when it is not required for access.
input_row.audit: From the Console: 1. Login to AWS Console using https://console.aws.amazon.com 2. Click All services, click Lightsail under Compute. 3. This will open up the Lightsail console. 4. Select the Windows or Linux Instance you want to review. 5. Go to the Networking section. 6. Under IPv6 networking confirm that it reads IPv6 networking is disabled. 7. If it reads IPv6 networking is enabled refer to the remediation below.
input_row.remediation: From the Console: 1. Login to AWS Console using https://console.aws.amazon.com 2. Click All services, click Lightsail under Compute. 3. This will open up the Lightsail console. 4. Select the Windows or Linux Instance you want to review. 5. Go to the Networking section. 6. Under IPv6 networking click on the check mark next to IPv6 networking is enabled. 7. In the Disable IPv6 for this instance? 8. Click on Yes, disable

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- The audit steps require human interaction with the AWS Console to verify the configuration in the Lightsail Networking section.
- Currently, there is no specific AWS API or CLI command available to directly check if IPv6 networking is enabled or disabled for Lightsail instances.
- The process involves visual confirmation which cannot be accomplished through API calls or any automated method.
- This task requires checking the console's Networking section to confirm the status of IPv6 networking, making it inherently a manual approach.

**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. **Login to AWS Console:**
   - Go to https://console.aws.amazon.com and sign in with your credentials.

2. **Navigate to Lightsail:**
   - Click on "All services" and select "Lightsail" under the "Compute" section to open the Lightsail Console.

3. **Select an Instance:**
   - Choose the specific Windows or Linux Lightsail instance you want to review.

4. **Access Networking Section:**
   - Go to the "Networking" section within the details of the selected instance.

5. **Verify IPv6 Networking Status:**
   - Check the status under "IPv6 networking."
   - Ensure it reads "IPv6 networking is disabled."

6. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - A secure configuration is indicated by IPv6 networking being disabled.
   - If IPv6 networking is enabled, it does not meet the control requirements and the remediation steps should be followed. 

These steps require a manual process as they involve visual confirmation and interaction with the AWS Console, which is why this control cannot be automated with current AWS APIs or CLI capabilities.
